svg.text: CATEGORY REVIEW ·
CONFIDENTIAL
Espresso category
· Q4 performance review
Prepared for Northstar Coffee · April 2026
What changed

text.tspan: in Q4, with private label gaining

svg.text: brands picking up the rest. The shift held across the top three banners, with Modern Trade showing the steepest
compression at −3.4 points. The decline is concentrated in the 250g multipack tier; the 1kg headline SKU held within
30 basis points of Q3.
The price-per-100g delta between branded and private label widened by 11 percent during the same window, while
branded distribution stayed flat at 87 percent ACV. Promotional depth on private label increased from 18 to 24 percent
off shelf, with no matching response from the branded tier. The pressure is on price-pack architecture, not on shelf
presence.
"The price-pack architecture is the lever, not the shelf-share conversation."
Why it matters
Three consecutive quarters of branded share decline have moved Northstar from a defensible posture to a structural one.
At the current rate of erosion, branded mainstream crosses below the 7.0-point floor by Q2 FY26, which is the threshold
at which two of the three banners renegotiate planogram allowances. The cost of inaction is not a soft Q1, but a binding
shelf-space conversation in May.
A targeted response on the 250g multipack: re-architecting the 4×250g and ×290g price ladders rather than discounting
the 1kg headline, recovers the lost points without conceding the premium anchor. Modeling against Q3 elasticity
suggests +1.4 to +1.8 points of recoverable share within two quarters, holding gross margin within 60 basis points of plan.
What we recommend
Hold the 1kg headline price. Respond on the 250g multipack architecture with a 4-pack at €9.90 and a 6-pack at €12.40,
anchored against the private-label 4×290g at €7.20.

A syllabus reference · refreshing weekly · last update Apr 18 2026
